While most existing approaches ignore the visual appearance-motion information at different temporal scales, it is unknown how to incorporate the multilevel processing capacity of a deep learning model with such multiscale information. Targeting these issues, this paper proposes a novel Multilevel Hierarchical Network (MHN) with multiscale sampling for VideoQA. MHN comprises two modules, namely Recurrent Multimodal Interaction (RMI) and Parallel Visual Reasoning (PVR). With a multiscale sampling, RMI iterates the interaction of appearance-motion information at each scale and the question embeddings to build the multilevel question-guided visual representations. Thereon, with a shared transformer encoder, PVR infers the visual cues at each level in parallel to fit with answering different question types that may rely on the visual information at relevant levels. Through extensive experiments on three VideoQA datasets, we demonstrate improved performances than previous state-of-the-arts and justify the effectiveness of each part of our method. 视频问答 (VideoQA) 具有视觉理解和自然语言处理的多模态组合，因此具有挑战性。虽然大多数现有方法忽略了不同时间尺度的视觉外观运动信息，但尚不清楚如何将深度学习模型的多级处理能力与此类多尺度信息结合起来。针对这些问题，本文提出了一种新颖的具有多尺度采样的多级分层网络（MHN）用于VideoQA。 MHN 包括两个模块，即循环多模态交互（RMI）和并行视觉推理（PVR）。通过多尺度采样，RMI 迭代每个尺度的外观运动信息和问题嵌入的交互，以构建多级问题引导的视觉表示。因此，通过共享变压器编码器，PVR 并行推断每个级别的视觉线索，以适应回答可能依赖于相关级别的视觉信息的不同问题类型。通过对三个 VideoQA 数据集进行广泛的实验，我们展示了比以前最先进的技术更高的性能，并证明了我们方法每个部分的有效性。 本文提出一种多级分层网络（MHN）结合多尺度采样方法，用于改进视频问答（VideoQA）任务中的视觉-语言信息融合。 Min Peng,Chongyang Wang,Yuan Gao,Yu Shi,Xiang-Dong Zhou cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Savvas Karatsiolis,Chirag Padubidri,Andreas Kamilaris
title: Exploiting Digital Surface Models for Inferring Super-Resolution for Remotely Sensed Images
title_zh: 利用数字表面模型推断遥感图像的超分辨率
abstract: Despite the plethora of successful Super-Resolution Reconstruction (SRR) models applied to natural images, their application to remote sensing imagery tends to produce poor results. Remote sensing imagery is often more complicated than natural images and has its peculiarities such as being of lower resolution, it contains noise, and often depicting large textured surfaces. As a result, applying non-specialized SRR models on remote sensing imagery results in artifacts and poor reconstructions. To address these problems, this paper proposes an architecture inspired by previous research work, introducing a novel approach for forcing an SRR model to output realistic remote sensing images: instead of relying on feature-space similarities as a perceptual loss, the model considers pixel-level information inferred from the normalized Digital Surface Model (nDSM) of the image. This strategy allows the application of better-informed updates during the training of the model which sources from a task (elevation map inference) that is closely related to remote sensing. Nonetheless, the nDSM auxiliary information is not required during production and thus the model infers a super-resolution image without any additional data besides its low-resolution pairs. We assess our model on two remotely sensed datasets of different spatial resolutions that also contain the DSM pairs of the images: the DFC2018 dataset and the dataset containing the national Lidar fly-by of Luxembourg. Based on visual inspection, the inferred super-resolution images exhibit particularly superior quality. In particular, the results for the high-resolution DFC2018 dataset are realistic and almost indistinguishable from the ground truth images.
summary: Despite the plethora of successful Super-Resolution Reconstruction (SRR) models applied to natural images, their application to remote sensing imagery tends to produce poor results. Remote sensing imagery is often more complicated than natural images and has its peculiarities such as being of lower resolution, it contains noise, and often depicting large textured surfaces. As a result, applying non-specialized SRR models on remote sensing imagery results in artifacts and poor reconstructions. To address these problems, this paper proposes an architecture inspired by previous research work, introducing a novel approach for forcing an SRR model to output realistic remote sensing images: instead of relying on feature-space similarities as a perceptual loss, the model considers pixel-level information inferred from the normalized Digital Surface Model (nDSM) of the image. This strategy allows the application of better-informed updates during the training of the model which sources from a task (elevation map inference) that is closely related to remote sensing. Nonetheless, the nDSM auxiliary information is not required during production and thus the model infers a super-resolution image without any additional data besides its low-resolution pairs. We assess our model on two remotely sensed datasets of different spatial resolutions that also contain the DSM pairs of the images: the DFC2018 dataset and the dataset containing the national Lidar fly-by of Luxembourg. Based on visual inspection, the inferred super-resolution images exhibit particularly superior quality. In particular, the results for the high-resolution DFC2018 dataset are realistic and almost indistinguishable from the ground truth images.
abstract_zh: 尽管有大量成功的超分辨率重建（SRR）模型应用于自然图像，但它们在遥感图像中的应用往往会产生较差的结果。遥感图像通常比自然图像更复杂，并且具有分辨率较低、包含噪声以及通常描绘大纹理表面等特点。因此，在遥感图像上应用非专业 SRR 模型会导致伪影和不良重建。为了解决这些问题，本文提出了一种受先前研究工作启发的架构，引入了一种强制 SRR 模型输出真实遥感图像的新方法：该模型不依赖特征空间相似性作为感知损失，而是考虑从图像的归一化数字表面模型 (nDSM) 推断出的像素级信息。该策略允许在模型训练期间应用更明智的更新，该更新源自与遥感密切相关的任务（高程图推断）。尽管如此，在生产过程中不需要 nDSM 辅助信息，因此该模型推断出超分辨率图像，除了低分辨率对之外，无需任何其他数据。我们在两个不同空间分辨率的遥感数据集上评估我们的模型，这两个数据集也包含图像的 DSM 对：DFC2018 数据集和包含卢森堡国家激光雷达飞越的数据集。基于目视检查，推断出的超分辨率图像表现出特别优异的质量。特别是，高分辨率 DFC2018 数据集的结果非常真实，与地面真实图像几乎无法区分。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用归一化数字高程模型（nDSM）作为感知损失，改进遥感图像的超分辨率重建（SRR）方法。
AI.motivation: 现有SRR模型在遥感图像上效果不佳，因其低分辨率、噪声和复杂纹理特性导致重建质量差，需针对性优化。
AI.method: 设计基于nDSM像素级信息的新型感知损失函数，训练时利用nDSM辅助，推理时无需额外数据，仅依赖低分辨率图像对。
AI.result: 在DFC2018和卢森堡LiDAR数据集上验证，生成图像视觉质量显著提升，高分辨率数据结果接近真实图像。
AI.conclusion: nDSM引导的SRR方法有效提升遥感图像超分质量，通过关联高程信息优化重建过程，且保持推理阶段的数据独立性。

search_text: Exploiting Digital Surface Models for Inferring Super-Resolution for Remotely Sensed Images 利用数字表面模型推断遥感图像的超分辨率 Despite the plethora of successful Super-Resolution Reconstruction (SRR) models applied to natural images, their application to remote sensing imagery tends to produce poor results. Remote sensing imagery is often more complicated than natural images and has its peculiarities such as being of lower resolution, it contains noise, and often depicting large textured surfaces. As a result, applying non-specialized SRR models on remote sensing imagery results in artifacts and poor reconstructions. To address these problems, this paper proposes an architecture inspired by previous research work, introducing a novel approach for forcing an SRR model to output realistic remote sensing images: instead of relying on feature-space similarities as a perceptual loss, the model considers pixel-level information inferred from the normalized Digital Surface Model (nDSM) of the image. This strategy allows the application of better-informed updates during the training of the model which sources from a task (elevation map inference) that is closely related to remote sensing. Nonetheless, the nDSM auxiliary information is not required during production and thus the model infers a super-resolution image without any additional data besides its low-resolution pairs. We assess our model on two remotely sensed datasets of different spatial resolutions that also contain the DSM pairs of the images: the DFC2018 dataset and the dataset containing the national Lidar fly-by of Luxembourg. Based on visual inspection, the inferred super-resolution images exhibit particularly superior quality. In particular, the results for the high-resolution DFC2018 dataset are realistic and almost indistinguishable from the ground truth images. 尽管有大量成功的超分辨率重建（SRR）模型应用于自然图像，但它们在遥感图像中的应用往往会产生较差的结果。遥感图像通常比自然图像更复杂，并且具有分辨率较低、包含噪声以及通常描绘大纹理表面等特点。因此，在遥感图像上应用非专业 SRR 模型会导致伪影和不良重建。为了解决这些问题，本文提出了一种受先前研究工作启发的架构，引入了一种强制 SRR 模型输出真实遥感图像的新方法：该模型不依赖特征空间相似性作为感知损失，而是考虑从图像的归一化数字表面模型 (nDSM) 推断出的像素级信息。该策略允许在模型训练期间应用更明智的更新，该更新源自与遥感密切相关的任务（高程图推断）。尽管如此，在生产过程中不需要 nDSM 辅助信息，因此该模型推断出超分辨率图像，除了低分辨率对之外，无需任何其他数据。我们在两个不同空间分辨率的遥感数据集上评估我们的模型，这两个数据集也包含图像的 DSM 对：DFC2018 数据集和包含卢森堡国家激光雷达飞越的数据集。基于目视检查，推断出的超分辨率图像表现出特别优异的质量。特别是，高分辨率 DFC2018 数据集的结果非常真实，与地面真实图像几乎无法区分。 本文提出利用归一化数字高程模型（nDSM）作为感知损失，改进遥感图像的超分辨率重建（SRR）方法。 Savvas Karatsiolis,Chirag Padubidri,Andreas Kamilaris cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Yanran Wang,Tatsuya Baba,Takashi Hikihara
title: Robot formation control in nonlinear manifold using Koopman operator theory
title_zh: 基于库普曼算子理论的非线性流形机器人编队控制
abstract: Formation control of multi-agent systems has been a prominent research topic, spanning both theoretical and practical domains over the past two decades. Our study delves into the leader-follower framework, addressing two critical, previously overlooked aspects. Firstly, we investigate the impact of an unknown nonlinear manifold, introducing added complexity to the formation control challenge. Secondly, we address the practical constraint of limited follower sensing range, posing difficulties in accurately localizing the leader for followers. Our core objective revolves around employing Koopman operator theory and Extended Dynamic Mode Decomposition to craft a reliable prediction algorithm for the follower robot to anticipate the leader's position effectively. Our experimentation on an elliptical paraboloid manifold, utilizing two omni-directional wheeled robots, validates the prediction algorithm's effectiveness.
summary: Formation control of multi-agent systems has been a prominent research topic, spanning both theoretical and practical domains over the past two decades. Our study delves into the leader-follower framework, addressing two critical, previously overlooked aspects. Firstly, we investigate the impact of an unknown nonlinear manifold, introducing added complexity to the formation control challenge. Secondly, we address the practical constraint of limited follower sensing range, posing difficulties in accurately localizing the leader for followers. Our core objective revolves around employing Koopman operator theory and Extended Dynamic Mode Decomposition to craft a reliable prediction algorithm for the follower robot to anticipate the leader's position effectively. Our experimentation on an elliptical paraboloid manifold, utilizing two omni-directional wheeled robots, validates the prediction algorithm's effectiveness.
abstract_zh: 在过去的二十年里，多智能体系统的编队控制一直是一个重要的研究课题，跨越了理论和实践领域。我们的研究深入研究了领导者-追随者框架，解决了两个关键的、以前被忽视的方面。首先，我们研究未知非线性流形的影响，从而增加编队控制挑战的复杂性。其次，我们解决了追随者感知范围有限的实际限制，这给追随者准确定位领导者带来了困难。我们的核心目标围绕采用库夫曼算子理论和扩展动态模式分解来为跟随机器人制定可靠的预测算法，以有效地预测领导者的位置。我们利用两个全向轮式机器人对椭圆抛物面流形进行实验，验证了预测算法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了多智能体系统在非线性流形上的编队控制问题，提出基于Koopman算子理论和扩展动态模式分解的预测算法以解决领导者定位难题。
AI.motivation: 现有编队控制方法未能有效处理未知非线性流形带来的复杂性及跟随者感知范围限制导致的领导者定位不准确问题。
AI.method: 结合Koopman算子理论与扩展动态模式分解（EDMD），构建能够预测领导者位置的算法，通过椭圆抛物面流形上的双全向轮机器人实验验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明所提出算法能有效预测领导者位置，成功实现非线性流形环境下的机器人编队控制。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为处理复杂流形和感知限制场景下的多机器人编队控制提供了新思路，验证了Koopman理论在控制问题中的应用潜力。

authors_text: Yan-Ran,Wang,Liangqiong Qu,Natasha Diba Sheybani,Xiaolong Luo,Jiangshan Wang,Kristina Elizabeth Hawk,Ashok Joseph Theruvath,Sergios Gatidis,Xuerong Xiao,Allison Pribnow,Daniel Rubin,Heike E. Daldrup-Link
title: Masked Co-attentional Transformer reconstructs 100x ultra-fast/low-dose whole-body PET from longitudinal images and anatomically guided MRI
title_zh: Masked Co-attentional Transformer 根据纵向图像和解剖引导 MRI 重建 100 倍超快/低剂量全身 PET
comment: This submission has been removed by arXiv administrators because the submitter did not have the right to assign the license at the time of submission
comments: This submission has been removed by arXiv administrators because the submitter did not have the right to assign the license at the time of submission
abstract: Despite its tremendous value for the diagnosis, treatment monitoring and surveillance of children with cancer, whole body staging with positron emission tomography (PET) is time consuming and associated with considerable radiation exposure. 100x (1% of the standard clinical dosage) ultra-low-dose/ultra-fast whole-body PET reconstruction has the potential for cancer imaging with unprecedented speed and improved safety, but it cannot be achieved by the naive use of machine learning techniques. In this study, we utilize the global similarity between baseline and follow-up PET and magnetic resonance (MR) images to develop Masked-LMCTrans, a longitudinal multi-modality co-attentional CNN-Transformer that provides interaction and joint reasoning between serial PET/MRs of the same patient. We mask the tumor area in the referenced baseline PET and reconstruct the follow-up PET scans. In this manner, Masked-LMCTrans reconstructs 100x almost-zero radio-exposure whole-body PET that was not possible before. The technique also opens a new pathway for longitudinal radiology imaging reconstruction, a significantly under-explored area to date. Our model was trained and tested with Stanford PET/MRI scans of pediatric lymphoma patients and evaluated externally on PET/MRI images from Tübingen University. The high image quality of the reconstructed 100x whole-body PET images resulting from the application of Masked-LMCTrans will substantially advance the development of safer imaging approaches and shorter exam-durations for pediatric patients, as well as expand the possibilities for frequent longitudinal monitoring of these patients by PET.
summary: Despite its tremendous value for the diagnosis, treatment monitoring and surveillance of children with cancer, whole body staging with positron emission tomography (PET) is time consuming and associated with considerable radiation exposure. 100x (1% of the standard clinical dosage) ultra-low-dose/ultra-fast whole-body PET reconstruction has the potential for cancer imaging with unprecedented speed and improved safety, but it cannot be achieved by the naive use of machine learning techniques. In this study, we utilize the global similarity between baseline and follow-up PET and magnetic resonance (MR) images to develop Masked-LMCTrans, a longitudinal multi-modality co-attentional CNN-Transformer that provides interaction and joint reasoning between serial PET/MRs of the same patient. We mask the tumor area in the referenced baseline PET and reconstruct the follow-up PET scans. In this manner, Masked-LMCTrans reconstructs 100x almost-zero radio-exposure whole-body PET that was not possible before. The technique also opens a new pathway for longitudinal radiology imaging reconstruction, a significantly under-explored area to date. Our model was trained and tested with Stanford PET/MRI scans of pediatric lymphoma patients and evaluated externally on PET/MRI images from Tübingen University. The high image quality of the reconstructed 100x whole-body PET images resulting from the application of Masked-LMCTrans will substantially advance the development of safer imaging approaches and shorter exam-durations for pediatric patients, as well as expand the possibilities for frequent longitudinal monitoring of these patients by PET.
abstract_zh: 尽管正电子发射断层扫描 (PET) 对癌症儿童的诊断、治疗监测和监测具有巨大价值，但其全身分期非常耗时，并且与大量辐射暴露相关。 100x（标准临床剂量的1%）超低剂量/超快速全身PET重建具有以前所未有的速度和更高的安全性进行癌症成像的潜力，但它无法通过简单地使用机器学习技术来实现。在本研究中，我们利用基线和后续 PET 和磁共振 (MR) 图像之间的全局相似性来开发 Masked-LMCTrans，这是一种纵向多模态共同注意 CNN-Transformer，可提供同一患者的连续 PET/MR 之间的交互和联合推理。我们在参考基线 PET 中掩盖肿瘤区域并重建后续 PET 扫描。通过这种方式，Masked-LMCTrans 重建了 100 倍几乎零辐射暴露的全身 PET，这在以前是不可能的。该技术还为纵向放射成像重建开辟了一条新途径，这是迄今为止尚未充分探索的领域。我们的模型通过儿科淋巴瘤患者的斯坦福 PET/MRI 扫描进行训练和测试，并根据蒂宾根大学的 PET/MRI 图像进行外部评估。应用 Masked-LMCTrans 所产生的重建 100 倍全身 PET 图像的高图像质量将大大促进更安全的成像方法的开发和更短的儿科患者检查时间，并扩大通过 PET 对这些患者进行频繁纵向监测的可能性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于纵向影像和解剖引导MRI的Masked Co-attentional Transformer模型，实现100倍超低剂量全身PET图像重建。
AI.motivation: 全身PET检查对儿童癌症患者具有重要价值，但存在检查时间长和辐射剂量高的问题，传统机器学习方法难以实现超低剂量重建。
AI.method: 利用基线与随访PET/MRI影像的全局相似性，开发纵向多模态共注意CNN-Transformer模型Masked-LMCTrans，通过掩码基线PET的肿瘤区域并重建随访PET扫描实现超低剂量成像。
AI.result: 在斯坦福和图宾根大学的儿科淋巴瘤数据集上验证，重建的100倍超低剂量PET图像质量高，显著提升影像安全性并缩短检查时间。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为纵向影像重建开辟新途径，可推动儿科患者更安全的影像检查和高频次随访监测。

The technique also opens a new pathway for longitudinal radiology imaging reconstruction, a significantly under-explored area to date. The high image quality of the reconstructed 100x whole-body PET images resulting from the application of Masked-LMCTrans will substantially advance the development of safer imaging approaches and shorter exam-durations for pediatric patients, as well as expand the possibilities for frequent longitudinal monitoring of these patients by PET. 尽管正电子发射断层扫描 (PET) 对癌症儿童的诊断、治疗监测和监测具有巨大价值，但其全身分期非常耗时，并且与大量辐射暴露相关。 100x（标准临床剂量的1%）超低剂量/超快速全身PET重建具有以前所未有的速度和更高的安全性进行癌症成像的潜力，但它无法通过简单地使用机器学习技术来实现。在本研究中，我们利用基线和后续 PET 和磁共振 (MR) 图像之间的全局相似性来开发 Masked-LMCTrans，这是一种纵向多模态共同注意 CNN-Transformer，可提供同一患者的连续 PET/MR 之间的交互和联合推理。我们在参考基线 PET 中掩盖肿瘤区域并重建后续 PET 扫描。通过这种方式，Masked-LMCTrans 重建了 100 倍几乎零辐射暴露的全身 PET，这在以前是不可能的。该技术还为纵向放射成像重建开辟了一条新途径，这是迄今为止尚未充分探索的领域。我们的模型通过儿科淋巴瘤患者的斯坦福 PET/MRI 扫描进行训练和测试，并根据蒂宾根大学的 PET/MRI 图像进行外部评估。应用 Masked-LMCTrans 所产生的重建 100 倍全身 PET 图像的高图像质量将大大促进更安全的成像方法的开发和更短的儿科患者检查时间，并扩大通过 PET 对这些患者进行频繁纵向监测的可能性。 本文提出一种基于纵向影像和解剖引导MRI的Masked Co-attentional Transformer模型，实现100倍超低剂量全身PET图像重建。 Yan-Ran,Wang,Liangqiong Qu,Natasha Diba Sheybani,Xiaolong Luo,Jiangshan Wang,Kristina Elizabeth Hawk,Ashok Joseph Theruvath,Sergios Gatidis,Xuerong Xiao,Allison Pribnow,Daniel Rubin,Heike E. Daldrup-Link eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Na Dong,Yongqiang Zhang,Mingli Ding,Gim Hee Lee
title: Incremental-DETR: Incremental Few-Shot Object Detection via Self-Supervised Learning
title_zh: Incremental-DETR：通过自监督学习进行增量少样本目标检测
abstract: Incremental few-shot object detection aims at detecting novel classes without forgetting knowledge of the base classes with only a few labeled training data from the novel classes. Most related prior works are on incremental object detection that rely on the availability of abundant training samples per novel class that substantially limits the scalability to real-world setting where novel data can be scarce. In this paper, we propose the Incremental-DETR that does incremental few-shot object detection via fine-tuning and self-supervised learning on the DETR object detector. To alleviate severe over-fitting with few novel class data, we first fine-tune the class-specific components of DETR with self-supervision from additional object proposals generated using Selective Search as pseudo labels. We further introduce an incremental few-shot fine-tuning strategy with knowledge distillation on the class-specific components of DETR to encourage the network in detecting novel classes without forgetting the base classes. Extensive experiments conducted on standard incremental object detection and incremental few-shot object detection settings show that our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods by a large margin.
summary: Incremental few-shot object detection aims at detecting novel classes without forgetting knowledge of the base classes with only a few labeled training data from the novel classes. Most related prior works are on incremental object detection that rely on the availability of abundant training samples per novel class that substantially limits the scalability to real-world setting where novel data can be scarce. In this paper, we propose the Incremental-DETR that does incremental few-shot object detection via fine-tuning and self-supervised learning on the DETR object detector. To alleviate severe over-fitting with few novel class data, we first fine-tune the class-specific components of DETR with self-supervision from additional object proposals generated using Selective Search as pseudo labels. We further introduce an incremental few-shot fine-tuning strategy with knowledge distillation on the class-specific components of DETR to encourage the network in detecting novel classes without forgetting the base classes. Extensive experiments conducted on standard incremental object detection and incremental few-shot object detection settings show that our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods by a large margin.
abstract_zh: 增量少样本目标检测旨在检测新类，而不会忘记基类的知识，而仅使用新类中的少量标记训练数据。大多数相关的先前工作都是关于增量对象检测，这些工作依赖于每个新类的丰富训练样本的可用性，这大大限制了新数据稀缺的现实环境的可扩展性。在本文中，我们提出了增量 DETR，它通过 DETR 目标检测器上的微调和自监督学习来进行增量几次目标检测。为了缓解少数新类数据的严重过度拟合，我们首先通过使用选择性搜索作为伪标签生成的附加对象建议进行自我监督，对 DETR 的特定于类的组件进行微调。我们进一步引入了一种增量几次微调策略，对 DETR 的类特定组件进行知识蒸馏，以鼓励网络在不忘记基类的情况下检测新类。对标准增量对象检测和增量少样本对象检测设置进行的大量实验表明，我们的方法大大优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种增量少样本目标检测方法，通过自监督学习和微调，在少量新类别数据下实现检测，同时避免遗忘已有类别知识。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖大量新类别样本，限制了在实际场景中的应用，因此需要一种在少量数据下有效的方法。
AI.method: 基于DETR检测器，结合自监督学习微调类别组件，并使用知识蒸馏策略防止遗忘，利用Selective Search生成伪标签减少过拟合。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在增量目标检测和少样本检测任务中显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法有效解决了增量少样本检测中的挑战，为实际应用提供了可行的解决方案。

authors_text: Xue Han,Zihuai Lin
title: AI Based Digital Twin Model for Cattle Caring
title_zh: 基于人工智能的牛护理数字孪生模型
abstract: In this paper, we developed innovative digital twins of cattle status that are powered by artificial intelligence (AI). The work was built on a farm IoT system that remotely monitors and tracks the state of cattle. A digital twin model of cattle health based on Deep Learning (DL) was generated using the sensor data acquired from the farm IoT system. The health and physiological cycle of cattle can be monitored in real time, and the state of the next physiological cycle of cattle can be anticipated using this model. The basis of this work is the vast amount of data which is required to validate the legitimacy of the digital twins model. In terms of behavioural state, it was found that the cattle treated with a combination of topical anaesthetic and meloxicam exhibits the least pain reaction. The digital twins model developed in this work can be used to monitor the health of cattle
summary: In this paper, we developed innovative digital twins of cattle status that are powered by artificial intelligence (AI). The work was built on a farm IoT system that remotely monitors and tracks the state of cattle. A digital twin model of cattle health based on Deep Learning (DL) was generated using the sensor data acquired from the farm IoT system. The health and physiological cycle of cattle can be monitored in real time, and the state of the next physiological cycle of cattle can be anticipated using this model. The basis of this work is the vast amount of data which is required to validate the legitimacy of the digital twins model. In terms of behavioural state, it was found that the cattle treated with a combination of topical anaesthetic and meloxicam exhibits the least pain reaction. The digital twins model developed in this work can be used to monitor the health of cattle
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们开发了由人工智能 (AI) 驱动的牛状况的创新数字双胞胎。这项工作建立在农场物联网系统的基础上，该系统可以远程监控和跟踪牛的状态。使用从农场物联网系统获取的传感器数据生成基于深度学习 (DL) 的牛健康数字孪生模型。该模型可以实时监测牛的健康状况和生理周期，并预测牛下一个生理周期的状态。这项工作的基础是验证数字孪生模型的合法性所需的大量数据。在行为状态方面，发现使用表面麻醉剂和美洛昔康联合治疗的牛表现出最少的疼痛反应。这项工作中开发的数字孪生模型可用于监测牛的健康状况

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于人工智能的数字孪生模型，用于实时监控和预测牲畜健康状况及生理周期。
AI.motivation: 通过实时监测和预测牲畜健康状态，提升畜牧业管理效率，减少疾病风险并优化养殖策略。
AI.method: 基于农场物联网系统采集的传感器数据，利用深度学习构建牲畜健康数字孪生模型，并通过行为实验验证不同镇痛方案的效果。
AI.result: 实现了牲畜健康状态的实时监控与生理周期预测，发现局部麻醉剂与美洛昔康联合使用可显著降低牲畜疼痛反应。
AI.conclusion: 该数字孪生模型为畜牧业提供了有效的健康管理工具，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Jiatong Shi,Shuai Guo,Tao Qian,Nan Huo,Tomoki Hayashi,Yuning Wu,Frank Xu,Xuankai Chang,Huazhe Li,Peter Wu,Shinji Watanabe,Qin Jin
title: Muskits: an End-to-End Music Processing Toolkit for Singing Voice Synthesis
title_zh: Muskits：用于歌声合成的端到端音乐处理工具包
abstract: This paper introduces a new open-source platform named Muskits for end-to-end music processing, which mainly focuses on end-to-end singing voice synthesis (E2E-SVS). Muskits supports state-of-the-art SVS models, including RNN SVS, transformer SVS, and XiaoiceSing. The design of Muskits follows the style of widely-used speech processing toolkits, ESPnet and Kaldi, for data prepossessing, training, and recipe pipelines. To the best of our knowledge, this toolkit is the first platform that allows a fair and highly-reproducible comparison between several published works in SVS. In addition, we also demonstrate several advanced usages based on the toolkit functionalities, including multilingual training and transfer learning. This paper describes the major framework of Muskits, its functionalities, and experimental results in single-singer, multi-singer, multilingual, and transfer learning scenarios. The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/SJTMusicTeam/Muskits.
summary: This paper introduces a new open-source platform named Muskits for end-to-end music processing, which mainly focuses on end-to-end singing voice synthesis (E2E-SVS). Muskits supports state-of-the-art SVS models, including RNN SVS, transformer SVS, and XiaoiceSing. The design of Muskits follows the style of widely-used speech processing toolkits, ESPnet and Kaldi, for data prepossessing, training, and recipe pipelines. To the best of our knowledge, this toolkit is the first platform that allows a fair and highly-reproducible comparison between several published works in SVS. In addition, we also demonstrate several advanced usages based on the toolkit functionalities, including multilingual training and transfer learning. This paper describes the major framework of Muskits, its functionalities, and experimental results in single-singer, multi-singer, multilingual, and transfer learning scenarios. The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/SJTMusicTeam/Muskits.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为Muskits的新型端到端音乐处理开源平台，该平台主要关注端到端歌声合成（E2E-SVS）。 Muskits支持最先进的SVS模型，包括RNN SVS、transformer SVS和XiaoiceSing。 Muskits 的设计遵循广泛使用的语音处理工具包 ESPnet 和 Kaldi 的风格，用于数据预置、训练和配方管道。据我们所知，该工具包是第一个可以在 SVS 中对多篇已发表作品进行公平且高度可重复比较的平台。此外，我们还演示了基于工具包功能的几种高级用法，包括多语言训练和迁移学习。本文描述了 Muskits 的主要框架、功能以及在单歌手、多歌手、多语言和迁移学习场景中的实验结果。该工具包可在 https://github.com/SJTMusicTeam/Muskits 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为Muskits的开源工具包，专注于端到端歌唱语音合成（E2E-SVS），支持多种先进模型并提供可复现的比较平台。
AI.motivation: 现有SVS模型缺乏统一的比较平台，导致实验结果难以复现，且多语言和迁移学习等高级功能支持不足。
AI.method: 基于ESPnet和Kaldi设计数据预处理、训练及流程框架，集成RNN、Transformer和XiaoiceSing等模型，并支持多语言训练与迁移学习。
AI.result: 在单歌手、多歌手、多语言及迁移学习场景中验证了工具包的有效性，实现了对现有工作的公平比较。
AI.conclusion: Muskits是首个支持多种SVS模型公平比较的工具包，通过高级功能演示展示了其在实际应用中的灵活性与实用性。

search_text: Muskits: an End-to-End Music Processing Toolkit for Singing Voice Synthesis Muskits：用于歌声合成的端到端音乐处理工具包 This paper introduces a new open-source platform named Muskits for end-to-end music processing, which mainly focuses on end-to-end singing voice synthesis (E2E-SVS). Muskits supports state-of-the-art SVS models, including RNN SVS, transformer SVS, and XiaoiceSing. The design of Muskits follows the style of widely-used speech processing toolkits, ESPnet and Kaldi, for data prepossessing, training, and recipe pipelines. To the best of our knowledge, this toolkit is the first platform that allows a fair and highly-reproducible comparison between several published works in SVS. In addition, we also demonstrate several advanced usages based on the toolkit functionalities, including multilingual training and transfer learning. This paper describes the major framework of Muskits, its functionalities, and experimental results in single-singer, multi-singer, multilingual, and transfer learning scenarios. The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/SJTMusicTeam/Muskits. 本文介绍了一种名为Muskits的新型端到端音乐处理开源平台，该平台主要关注端到端歌声合成（E2E-SVS）。 Muskits支持最先进的SVS模型，包括RNN SVS、transformer SVS和XiaoiceSing。 Muskits 的设计遵循广泛使用的语音处理工具包 ESPnet 和 Kaldi 的风格，用于数据预置、训练和配方管道。据我们所知，该工具包是第一个可以在 SVS 中对多篇已发表作品进行公平且高度可重复比较的平台。此外，我们还演示了基于工具包功能的几种高级用法，包括多语言训练和迁移学习。本文描述了 Muskits 的主要框架、功能以及在单歌手、多歌手、多语言和迁移学习场景中的实验结果。该工具包可在 https://github.com/SJTMusicTeam/Muskits 上公开获取。 本文介绍了一个名为Muskits的开源工具包，专注于端到端歌唱语音合成（E2E-SVS），支持多种先进模型并提供可复现的比较平台。 Jiatong Shi,Shuai Guo,Tao Qian,Nan Huo,Tomoki Hayashi,Yuning Wu,Frank Xu,Xuankai Chang,Huazhe Li,Peter Wu,Shinji Watanabe,Qin Jin cs.SD,cs.MM,eess.AS

authors_text: Chilam Cheang,Haitao Lin,Yanwei Fu,Xiangyang Xue
title: Learning 6-DoF Object Poses to Grasp Category-level Objects by Language Instructions
title_zh: 通过语言指令学习 6-DoF 物体姿势以抓取类别级物体
abstract: This paper studies the task of any objects grasping from the known categories by free-form language instructions. This task demands the technique in computer vision, natural language processing, and robotics. We bring these disciplines together on this open challenge, which is essential to human-robot interaction. Critically, the key challenge lies in inferring the category of objects from linguistic instructions and accurately estimating the 6-DoF information of unseen objects from the known classes. In contrast, previous works focus on inferring the pose of object candidates at the instance level. This significantly limits its applications in real-world scenarios.In this paper, we propose a language-guided 6-DoF category-level object localization model to achieve robotic grasping by comprehending human intention. To this end, we propose a novel two-stage method. Particularly, the first stage grounds the target in the RGB image through language description of names, attributes, and spatial relations of objects. The second stage extracts and segments point clouds from the cropped depth image and estimates the full 6-DoF object pose at category-level. Under such a manner, our approach can locate the specific object by following human instructions, and estimate the full 6-DoF pose of a category-known but unseen instance which is not utilized for training the model. Extensive experimental results show that our method is competitive with the state-of-the-art language-conditioned grasp method. Importantly, we deploy our approach on a physical robot to validate the usability of our framework in real-world applications. Please refer to the supplementary for the demo videos of our robot experiments.

This task demands the technique in computer vision, natural language processing, and robotics. We bring these disciplines together on this open challenge, which is essential to human-robot interaction. Critically, the key challenge lies in inferring the category of objects from linguistic instructions and accurately estimating the 6-DoF information of unseen objects from the known classes. In contrast, previous works focus on inferring the pose of object candidates at the instance level. This significantly limits its applications in real-world scenarios.In this paper, we propose a language-guided 6-DoF category-level object localization model to achieve robotic grasping by comprehending human intention. To this end, we propose a novel two-stage method. Particularly, the first stage grounds the target in the RGB image through language description of names, attributes, and spatial relations of objects. The second stage extracts and segments point clouds from the cropped depth image and estimates the full 6-DoF object pose at category-level. Under such a manner, our approach can locate the specific object by following human instructions, and estimate the full 6-DoF pose of a category-known but unseen instance which is not utilized for training the model. Extensive experimental results show that our method is competitive with the state-of-the-art language-conditioned grasp method. Importantly, we deploy our approach on a physical robot to validate the usability of our framework in real-world applications. Please refer to the supplementary for the demo videos of our robot experiments.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了通过自由形式语言指令从已知类别中抓取任何对象的任务。这项任务需要计算机视觉、自然语言处理和机器人技术。我们将这些学科聚集在一起应对这一公开挑战，这对于人机交互至关重要。至关重要的是，关键的挑战在于从语言指令推断物体的类别，并从已知类别中准确估计不可见物体的 6-DoF 信息。相比之下，以前的工作侧重于在实例级别推断候选对象的姿势。这极大地限制了其在现实场景中的应用。在本文中，我们提出了一种语言引导的6-DoF类别级对象定位模型，以通过理解人类意图来实现机器人抓取。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的两阶段方法。具体来说，第一阶段通过对象的名称、属性和空间关系的语言描述将目标置于RGB图像中。第二阶段从裁剪后的深度图像中提取和分割点云，并估计类别级别的完整 6-DoF 对象姿态。在这种方式下，我们的方法可以通过遵循人类指令来定位特定对象，并估计类别已知但未见过的实例的完整 6-DoF 姿态，该实例不用于训练模型。大量的实验结果表明，我们的方法与最先进的语言条件掌握方法具有竞争力。重要的是，我们在物理机器人上部署我们的方法，以验证我们的框架在实际应用中的可用性。我们的机器人实验演示视频请参阅补充。

AI.tldr: 本文研究通过自由形式语言指令抓取已知类别物体的任务，提出语言引导的6-DoF类别级物体定位模型。
AI.motivation: 现有方法聚焦于实例级物体位姿推断，难以满足实际场景中对类别级未知物体抓取的需求。
AI.method: 提出两阶段方法：第一阶段通过语言描述在RGB图像中定位目标，第二阶段利用深度图像点云估计类别级6-DoF位姿。
AI.result: 实验表明方法性能优于现有最佳语言条件抓取方法，并成功部署于物理机器人验证实际应用效果。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效实现了人类意图理解与机器人抓取的结合，推动了人机交互技术发展。

authors_text: Haitao Lin,Chilam Cheang,Yanwei Fu,Xiangyang Xue
title: I Know What You Draw: Learning Grasp Detection Conditioned on a Few Freehand Sketches
title_zh: 我知道你画什么：以一些手绘草图为条件学习抓取检测
abstract: In this paper, we are interested in the problem of generating target grasps by understanding freehand sketches. The sketch is useful for the persons who cannot formulate language and the cases where a textual description is not available on the fly. However, very few works are aware of the usability of this novel interactive way between humans and robots. To this end, we propose a method to generate a potential grasp configuration relevant to the sketch-depicted objects. Due to the inherent ambiguity of sketches with abstract details, we take the advantage of the graph by incorporating the structure of the sketch to enhance the representation ability. This graph-represented sketch is further validated to improve the generalization of the network, capable of learning the sketch-queried grasp detection by using a small collection (around 100 samples) of hand-drawn sketches. Additionally, our model is trained and tested in an end-to-end manner which is easy to be implemented in real-world applications. Experiments on the multi-object VMRD and GraspNet-1Billion datasets demonstrate the good generalization of the proposed method. The physical robot experiments confirm the utility of our method in object-cluttered scenes.
summary: In this paper, we are interested in the problem of generating target grasps by understanding freehand sketches. The sketch is useful for the persons who cannot formulate language and the cases where a textual description is not available on the fly. However, very few works are aware of the usability of this novel interactive way between humans and robots. To this end, we propose a method to generate a potential grasp configuration relevant to the sketch-depicted objects. Due to the inherent ambiguity of sketches with abstract details, we take the advantage of the graph by incorporating the structure of the sketch to enhance the representation ability. This graph-represented sketch is further validated to improve the generalization of the network, capable of learning the sketch-queried grasp detection by using a small collection (around 100 samples) of hand-drawn sketches. Additionally, our model is trained and tested in an end-to-end manner which is easy to be implemented in real-world applications. Experiments on the multi-object VMRD and GraspNet-1Billion datasets demonstrate the good generalization of the proposed method. The physical robot experiments confirm the utility of our method in object-cluttered scenes.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们感兴趣的是通过理解手绘草图来生成目标抓取的问题。该草图对于无法表达语言的人和无法即时获得文本描述的情况很有用。然而，很少有作品意识到这种人与机器人之间新颖的交互方式的可用性。为此，我们提出了一种方法来生成与草图描绘的对象相关的潜在抓取配置。由于具有抽象细节的草图固有的模糊性，我们利用图形的优势，结合草图的结构来增强表达能力。这个图形表示的草图经过进一步验证，可以提高网络的泛化能力，能够通过使用少量手绘草图（大约 100 个样本）来学习草图查询抓取检测。此外，我们的模型以端到端的方式进行训练和测试，很容易在实际应用中实现。在多目标 VMRD 和 GraspNet-1Billion 数据集上的实验证明了该方法具有良好的泛化性。物理机器人实验证实了我们的方法在物体杂乱的场景中的实用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于少量自由手绘草图生成目标抓取配置的方法，用于机器人抓取任务。
AI.motivation: 草图作为非语言交互方式在机器人抓取中具有重要价值，但现有研究缺乏对草图引导抓取的有效方法，尤其在样本稀缺场景下。
AI.method: 构建图结构表示草图抽象特征，通过端到端网络学习草图到抓取配置的映射，仅需约100个手绘样本即可训练模型。
AI.result: 在VMRD和GraspNet-1Billion数据集上验证了方法泛化性，物理实验表明模型能在杂乱场景中有效执行抓取任务。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过图结构增强草图表征，在少量样本条件下实现了可落地的草图引导抓取检测，推动了人机交互式机器人技术发展。

conclusion: 该方法通过图结构增强草图表征，在少量样本条件下实现了可落地的草图引导抓取检测，推动了人机交互式机器人技术发展。
search_text: I Know What You Draw: Learning Grasp Detection Conditioned on a Few Freehand Sketches 我知道你画什么：以一些手绘草图为条件学习抓取检测 In this paper, we are interested in the problem of generating target grasps by understanding freehand sketches. The sketch is useful for the persons who cannot formulate language and the cases where a textual description is not available on the fly. However, very few works are aware of the usability of this novel interactive way between humans and robots. To this end, we propose a method to generate a potential grasp configuration relevant to the sketch-depicted objects. Due to the inherent ambiguity of sketches with abstract details, we take the advantage of the graph by incorporating the structure of the sketch to enhance the representation ability. This graph-represented sketch is further validated to improve the generalization of the network, capable of learning the sketch-queried grasp detection by using a small collection (around 100 samples) of hand-drawn sketches. Additionally, our model is trained and tested in an end-to-end manner which is easy to be implemented in real-world applications. Experiments on the multi-object VMRD and GraspNet-1Billion datasets demonstrate the good generalization of the proposed method. The physical robot experiments confirm the utility of our method in object-cluttered scenes. 在本文中，我们感兴趣的是通过理解手绘草图来生成目标抓取的问题。该草图对于无法表达语言的人和无法即时获得文本描述的情况很有用。然而，很少有作品意识到这种人与机器人之间新颖的交互方式的可用性。为此，我们提出了一种方法来生成与草图描绘的对象相关的潜在抓取配置。由于具有抽象细节的草图固有的模糊性，我们利用图形的优势，结合草图的结构来增强表达能力。这个图形表示的草图经过进一步验证，可以提高网络的泛化能力，能够通过使用少量手绘草图（大约 100 个样本）来学习草图查询抓取检测。此外，我们的模型以端到端的方式进行训练和测试，很容易在实际应用中实现。在多目标 VMRD 和 GraspNet-1Billion 数据集上的实验证明了该方法具有良好的泛化性。物理机器人实验证实了我们的方法在物体杂乱的场景中的实用性。 本文提出一种基于少量自由手绘草图生成目标抓取配置的方法，用于机器人抓取任务。 Haitao Lin,Chilam Cheang,Yanwei Fu,Xiangyang Xue cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Maria Nădejde,Anna Currey,Benjamin Hsu,Xing Niu,Marcello Federico,Georgiana Dinu
title: CoCoA-MT: A Dataset and Benchmark for Contrastive Controlled MT with Application to Formality
title_zh: CoCoA-MT：对比控制 MT 的数据集和基准及其形式化应用
abstract: The machine translation (MT) task is typically formulated as that of returning a single translation for an input segment. However, in many cases, multiple different translations are valid and the appropriate translation may depend on the intended target audience, characteristics of the speaker, or even the relationship between speakers. Specific problems arise when dealing with honorifics, particularly translating from English into languages with formality markers. For example, the sentence "Are you sure?" can be translated in German as "Sind Sie sich sicher?" (formal register) or "Bist du dir sicher?" (informal). Using wrong or inconsistent tone may be perceived as inappropriate or jarring for users of certain cultures and demographics. This work addresses the problem of learning to control target language attributes, in this case formality, from a small amount of labeled contrastive data. We introduce an annotated dataset (CoCoA-MT) and an associated evaluation metric for training and evaluating formality-controlled MT models for six diverse target languages. We show that we can train formality-controlled models by fine-tuning on labeled contrastive data, achieving high accuracy (82% in-domain and 73% out-of-domain) while maintaining overall quality.
summary: The machine translation (MT) task is typically formulated as that of returning a single translation for an input segment. However, in many cases, multiple different translations are valid and the appropriate translation may depend on the intended target audience, characteristics of the speaker, or even the relationship between speakers. Specific problems arise when dealing with honorifics, particularly translating from English into languages with formality markers. For example, the sentence "Are you sure?" can be translated in German as "Sind Sie sich sicher?" (formal register) or "Bist du dir sicher?" (informal). Using wrong or inconsistent tone may be perceived as inappropriate or jarring for users of certain cultures and demographics. This work addresses the problem of learning to control target language attributes, in this case formality, from a small amount of labeled contrastive data. We introduce an annotated dataset (CoCoA-MT) and an associated evaluation metric for training and evaluating formality-controlled MT models for six diverse target languages. We show that we can train formality-controlled models by fine-tuning on labeled contrastive data, achieving high accuracy (82% in-domain and 73% out-of-domain) while maintaining overall quality.
abstract_zh: 机器翻译 (MT) 任务通常被表述为返回输入片段的单个翻译。然而，在许多情况下，多种不同的翻译都是有效的，并且适当的翻译可能取决于预期的目标受众、说话者的特征，甚至说话者之间的关系。在处理敬语时会出现具体问题，特别是从英语翻译成带有正式标记的语言时。例如，“你确定吗？”这句话德语可以翻译为“Sind Sie sich sicher？” （正式注册）或“Bist du dir sicher？” （非正式）。对于某些文化和人口统计数据的用户来说，使用错误或不一致的语气可能会被认为是不合适或不和谐的。这项工作解决了从少量标记的对比数据中学习控制目标语言属性（在本例中为形式）的问题。我们引入了一个带注释的数据集 (CoCoA-MT) 和一个相关的评估指标，用于训练和评估六种不同目标语言的形式控制的 MT 模型。我们证明，我们可以通过对标记的对比数据进行微调来训练形式控制模型，在保持整体质量的同时实现高精度（域内 82%，域外 73%）。

AI.tldr: 提出CoCoA-MT数据集和基准，用于对比控制机器翻译中的正式程度控制。
AI.motivation: 传统机器翻译生成单一翻译，但实际需求需根据受众调整语气（如正式/非正式），错误语气可能引发文化不适。
AI.method: 构建标注对比数据集CoCoA-MT，设计评估指标，通过微调少量标注对比数据训练正式程度控制模型。
AI.result: 领域内准确率82%，领域外73%，同时保持整体翻译质量。
AI.conclusion: 证明通过微调对比数据可有效控制翻译正式程度，数据集和评估指标为相关研究提供基准。
